flashcards.back: Inheritance is a concept in object-oriented programming that allows one class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of inheritance in C++?
flashcards.back: Inheritance promotes code reuse, facilitates the creation of a hierarchy of related classes, and supports the 'is-a' relationship between classes.

flashcards.front: What keyword is used to implement inheritance in C++?
flashcards.back: The ':' keyword is used to implement inheritance in C++.

flashcards.front: What is a base class in C++?
flashcards.back: A base class is the class from which another class inherits its members.

flashcards.front: What is a derived class in C++?
flashcards.back: A derived class is the class that inherits the members of a base class.

flashcards.front: What is the 'is-a' relationship in C++?
flashcards.back: The 'is-a' relationship is a relationship between classes where a derived class is a specialization of a base class.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between public and private inheritance in C++?
flashcards.back: Public inheritance allows the derived class to access the public members of the base class, while private inheritance restricts access to the private members of the base class.

flashcards.front: Can a derived class inherit from multiple base classes in C++?
flashcards.back: Yes, a derived class can inherit from multiple base classes in C++ using multiple inheritance.

flashcards.front: What is the diamond problem in C++?
flashcards.back: The diamond problem is a problem that arises when a derived class inherits from multiple base classes that have a common base class, resulting in ambiguity.

flashcards.front: How is the diamond problem resolved in C++?
flashcards.back: The diamond problem is resolved using virtual inheritance, where the common base class is declared as a virtual base class.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between static and dynamic binding in C++?
flashcards.back: Static binding occurs at compile-time, while dynamic binding occurs at runtime.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the 'virtual' keyword in C++?
flashcards.back: The 'virtual' keyword is used to declare a virtual function, which can be overridden by a derived class.

flashcards.front: What is a pure virtual function in C++?
flashcards.back: A pure virtual function is a virtual function that must be implemented by a derived class.

flashcards.front: What is an abstract class in C++?
flashcards.back: An abstract class is a class that contains at least one pure virtual function and cannot be instantiated.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between an abstract class and an interface in C++?
flashcards.back: An abstract class can contain both pure virtual and non-virtual functions, while an interface can only contain pure virtual functions.
